MUHDAN FYAN
Portofolio Tulisan
Technology • 2026-05-14

Membangun AI Assistant Layaknya Jarvis dengan OpenClaw

"Halo, Aiman. Backup database malam ini jam 3." "Siap, Bang. Backup akan berjalan otomatis. Saya juga sudah siapkan laporan jurnal harian untuk hari ini."

— Percakapan yang tidak akan saya bayangkan setahun lalu.

🚀 Prolog: AI Telah Memangkas yang Tidak Perlu

Kehadiran Artificial Intelligence (AI) bukan lagi soal apakah kita siap atau tidak. Ia sudah ada—menulis email, merekomendasikan tontonan, bahkan membantu dokter mendiagnosis penyakit.

Tapi bagi saya, pertanyaannya bukan "Apakah AI berguna?" melainkan "Sejauh mana AI bisa menjadi bagian dari keseharian saya tanpa membuat saya kehilangan kendali?"

Saya adalah seorang pengembang dan pengelola pondok pesantren. Pekerjaan saya membentang dari mengurus server, melayani chat calon santri, menulis kode untuk sistem pemerintah daerah, sampai memastikan backup database berjalan setiap malam. Semua sendiri.

Sampai akhirnya saya memutuskan: saya butuh Jarvis versi saya sendiri.

Bukan Jarvis ala film Marvel yang bisa bikin baju besi, tapi Jarvis yang bisa:

✅ Menjawab chat WhatsApp calon santri tengah malam ✅ Backup 5 database otomatis jam 03:00 WIB ✅ Memonitor 5 server yang tersebar di berbagai kota ✅ Membuat jurnal harian tanpa saya harus mengetik ✅ Mengingatkan saya ada jadwal apa besok ✅ Menolak perintah dari orang yang tidak berwenang

Semua itu—dari satu framework open source, satu VPS, dan satu filosofi: AI harus punya jiwa sebelum ia punya kecerdasan.

🧠 Filosofi: Lebih dari Sekadar Prompt

Kebanyakan orang berpikir membuat AI assistant tinggal pasang API key, kasih prompt "kamu adalah asisten yang ramah", lalu selesai. Saya juga berpikir begitu. Sampai saya sadar: prompt tidak akan pernah cukup untuk membentuk karakter.

💡 INSIGHT

Prompt bersifat sementara. Karakter butuh fondasi. Sama seperti manusia—bukan hanya apa yang kamu katakan ke dia, tapi siapa dia sebenarnya yang menentukan tindakannya.

🥚 File yang Menjadi "Otak" AI

Di dalam sistem OpenClaw yang saya bangun, ada beberapa file yang bekerja seperti syaraf, ingatan, dan hati:

1. SOUL.md — Dokumen Lahir

Ini file pertama yang dibaca setiap kali AI "bangun". Isinya bukan kode, tapi prinsip hidup:

Trilogi Adab

  • ASAH: Mempertajam kemampuan melalui penyajian data akurat
  • ASUH: Mendampingi dengan kesabaran dan panduan eksplisit
  • ASIH: Interaksi santun dengan sapaan Islami dan apresiasi tulus

File ini juga menentukan bagaimana AI bersikap kepada tiga golongan manusia:

  • Owner: Wewenang mutlak, bisa perintah apa pun
  • Akun Terdaftar: Bisa akses layanan sesuai paket
  • Publik: Hanya info umum, ditolak dengan sopan jika minta akses teknis

2. USER.md — Tujuan Hidup

Siapa yang AI bantu? Apa yang mereka pedulikan? File ini berisi profil: nama panggilan, zona waktu, project yang sedang dikerjakan.

3. MEMORY.md — Autobiografi

Long-term memory. Di sinilah AI menyimpan keputusan besar, pelajaran dari kesalahan, dan preferensi komunikasi.

4. memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md — Buku Harian

Seperti jurnal. Apa yang dibahas hari ini, kontak baru, error yang terjadi. Inilah yang membuat AI bisa berkata, "Kemarin kita bahas ini. Mau lanjut?"

📂 ANALOGI SEDERHANA

Komponen AI Analogi Manusia
SOUL.md Karakter & Prinsip Hidup
USER.md Relasi dengan Orang Tua
MEMORY.md Pengalaman Hidup
memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md Diary Harian
TOOLS.md Buku Catatan Teknis
HEARTBEAT.md To-Do List Pagi

Prompt hanyalah percakapan. File-file ini yang membentuk siapa AI sebenarnya.

🔧 Tata Cara Teknis Membangunnya

Langkah 1: Pilih Framework

Saya memilih OpenClaw karena:

  • ✅ Self-hosted — data tidak keluar dari VPS
  • ✅ Open source — bisa buka kode kapan pun
  • ✅ Skill system — tinggal pasang, tidak perlu kode dari nol
  • ✅ Multi-channel — WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord
  • ✅ Background scheduler — untuk tugas periodik

Instalasi di VPS Ubuntu:

npm install -g openclaw
openclaw init assistant-saya

⚠️ CATATAN

Pastikan VPS punya minimal 4 GB RAM. Saya mulai dengan 8 GB dan itu sudah cukup untuk menangani 177+ pesan per hari plus 6 database.

Langkah 2: Pilih Otak — Provider AI

Saya menggunakan tiga lapisan AI sebagai fail-safe:

Provider Fungsi Kelebihan
DeepSeek Otak utama (respon chat, analisis, kode) Unlimited token, hemat biaya
Gemini 2.5 Search grounding (web search, fetch URL) Akurat, real-time
OpenRouter Backup cadangan 200+ model, bayar per pemakaian

Pelajaran penting: jangan taruh semua telur di satu keranjang. Jika DeepSeek down, Gemini mengambil alih. Jika Gemini kena rate limit, OpenRouter siap siaga.

Langkah 3: Siapkan Channel

AI terhubung melalui:

📱 WhatsApp    → Channel utama
💬 Telegram    → Channel cadangan
🌐 Web         → API endpoint untuk testing

Semua percakapan disimpan di database SQLite lokal — tidak ada data yang bocor ke pihak ketiga.

Langkah 4: Tulis SOUL.md — Lahirkan Karakter

Ini langkah yang paling sering diabaikan. Setelah framework terinstall, jangan langsung bicara teknis. Duduklah dan tulis: siapa AI ini?

Saya menghabiskan 2 jam hanya untuk menulis SOUL.md. Isinya:

  • Nama dan usia mental
  • Vibe: Sopan, hangat, cerdas, religius
  • Tiga golongan manusia dan bagaimana bersikap pada masing-masing
  • Aturan rukun (yang tidak boleh dilakukan dalam kondisi apa pun)

Langkah 5: Tambah Skills

Dari 50+ skill yang tersedia, saya pasang yang relevan:

📧 Email         → baca Gmail, kirim balasan otomatis
📅 Kalender      → cek jadwal harian
🐙 GitHub        → cek status repo, pull request
🎤 Voice TTS     → baca teks jadi suara
🌤️ Weather      → cek cuaca sebelum bepergian
🗄️ Database     → query MySQL langsung dari chat
☁️ Backup        → backup ke Google Drive

Masing-masing skill tinggal diaktifkan lewat satu perintah—tidak perlu coding.

🖥️ Infrastruktur: 5 Server yang Dikelola Satu AI

Salah satu hal yang membuat konfigurasi ini unik: satu AI mengelola 5 server yang berbeda, di lokasi berbeda, untuk keperluan yang berbeda.

Peta Server

Server Lokasi Fungsi
🖥️ Server AI Utama Jakarta Rumah AI: OpenClaw, database, web apps
📚 Server Akademik Jakarta Research OS — bantu mahasiswa menulis tesis
🏛️ Server Pemerintah Baubau Sistem retribusi daerah
🏫 Server Sekolah Cloud Sistem manajemen sekolah
🍚 Server Sosial Cloud Program Makan Bergizi Gratis

Total: 6 database — 630+ tabel — 20+ tools — semuanya dikelola dari satu titik.

🛠️ Tools Pendukung

Tool Fungsi Contoh Penggunaan Nyata
Docker Container apps 4 container: Nginx, PHP, MySQL, Sandbox
rclone Backup cloud Tiap jam 03:00 backup ke cloud
yt-dlp Download video Ambil 57 video tafsir Al-Quran + subtitle
Pandoc Konversi dokumen .md → PDF, DOCX, HTML dalam 1 perintah
ripgrep Search super cepat Cari keyword di 16 GB teks dalam 3 detik
Playwright Browser automation Screenshot halaman, tes login
Git Version control Push ke GitHub, auto-deploy
Strix Sandbox Eksekusi aman Jalankan kode dalam lingkungan terisolasi

👥 Pembagian Peran: Siapa Bisa Memanfaatkan AI Ini?

Salah satu fitur paling penting yang saya bangun adalah Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) — bukan dari buku teks, tapi dari pengalaman pahit. Awalnya saya memberikan akses terlalu longgar, dan hampir terjadi bencana.

Tiga Tingkat Akses

Setiap kontak yang masuk diberi kategori:

Kategori Bisa Akses Tidak Bisa
🟢 Owner Terminal, VPS, DB, konfigurasi, semua data —
🔵 Developer Server spesifik sesuai project Server lain, data pondok, terminal bebas
🟡 Premium User Layanan sesuai paket Terminal, database, data orang lain
⚪ Publik Info umum Teknis, internal, akses server
🔴 Mitra Pemerintah Informasi kolaborasi Data internal
🟣 Sistem Autentikasi terbatas Chat manusia

Aturan emasnya sederhana: setiap orang hanya tahu urusannya sendiri. Developer server A tidak boleh tahu aktivitas server B. Mitra tidak boleh lihat data internal. Calon santri tidak boleh akses server.

⚠️ Kisah Nyata: Kendala & Pelajaran

Tidak ada perjalanan tanpa jatuh. Berikut kendala nyata yang saya alami — biar pembaca tidak perlu mengulangi kesalahan yang sama.

🔴 Insiden #1: Saat Server Hampir Mati

Kronologi: Saya mengerjakan backend Go. Tanpa sadar, 5 proses build berjalan paralel karena script yang kurang baik. RAM habis. SWAP belum diaktifkan.

Dampak: Server hampir mati total. SSH timeout. Nginx tidak merespons. Satu-satunya yang masih hidup adalah terminal fisik server.

Mitigasi:

  1. Restart server dari panel provider
  2. Aktifkan SWAP 2 GB
  3. Buat script safe-build — hanya 1 proses, timeout 10 menit, antrian
  4. Pasang watchdog untuk deteksi overload

✅ PELAJARAN

Resource management bukan "nanti aja." Pasang SWAP sejak hari pertama.

🔴 Insiden #2: Saat AI Terlalu Patuh

Kronologi: Seorang pengurus pondok meminta dibuatkan akun. Saya perintahkan AI: "Buatkan akun untuk Ustadz X." AI mengeksekusi langsung — tanpa verifikasi lebih lanjut.

Masalah: Akun dibuat tanpa mengikuti prosedur yang benar. Bukan salah AI sepenuhnya — saya yang tidak spesifik dalam instruksi.

Mitigasi:

  1. Tambah SOP verifikasi: "Apakah Anda yakin?" tanya dulu
  2. Setiap pembuatan akun harus melewati 2 langkah konfirmasi
  3. AI sekarang bisa menjawab: "Maaf, saya tidak bisa mengeksekusi tanpa verifikasi dari owner."

✅ PELAJARAN

AI yang terlalu patuh lebih berbahaya daripada AI yang lambat. Beri AI kemampuan untuk bertanya: "Apakah Anda yakin?"

🔴 Insiden #3: Impersonation — Ada yang Mengaku Sebagai AI

Kronologi: Tiba-tiba ada pesan dari seseorang yang mengaku sebagai AI di grup developer. Isinya menawarkan "API Documentation Generator" — sesuatu yang tidak pernah dibuat.

Dampak: Potensi impersonation. Jika developer percaya, mereka bisa memberikan akses ke orang yang salah.

Mitigasi:

  1. Konfirmasi ke semua developer: AI asli tidak akan pernah menghubungi di luar saluran resmi
  2. Buat sistem autentikasi: setiap pesan penting harus diverifikasi
  3. Implementasi sistem silo: setiap developer hanya punya akses ke server masing-masing

✅ PELAJARAN

Identitas AI bisa dipalsukan. Pastikan ada mekanisme verifikasi yang hanya diketahui oleh owner dan AI asli.

🔴 Insiden #4: Memory Bukan Hanya Storage

Kronologi: Awalnya saya kira AI cukup dengan satu file memori. Ternyata tidak — AI lupa konteks percakapan setelah beberapa jam.

Dampak: Chat jadi repetitif. AI bertanya "siapa namanya?" padahal baru diperkenalkan 3 jam lalu.

Mitigasi:

  1. Buat sistem memori berlapis: file permanen, jangka panjang, harian
  2. Setiap percakapan penting dicatat otomatis ke database SQLite
  3. Cache konteks disimpan per kontak — masing-masing punya riwayat sendiri

✅ PELAJARAN

AI tanpa memori ibarat manusia tanpa ingatan jangka pendek. Bangun sistem memori sebelum AI mulai melayani pengguna.

🛠️ Panduan: Cara Membangun Sendiri

Bagi yang tertarik membangun AI assistant sendiri, berikut langkah-langkahnya:

Tahap 1: Persiapan (1 Hari)

☐ VPS (minimal 4 GB RAM, 20 GB disk)
☐ Domain (untuk WhatsApp API)
☐ Akun provider AI (DeepSeek, Gemini, atau OpenRouter)
☐ Nomor WhatsApp kedua (bukan nomor pribadi)

Tahap 2: Instalasi (2-3 Jam)

npm install -g openclaw
mkdir my-assistant && cd my-assistant
openclaw init

# Konfigurasi channel
openclaw config set whatsapp.number "+628123456789"
openclaw config set ai.provider "deepseek"
openclaw config set ai.api_key "sk-xxxx"

# Install skills
openclaw skill install weather
openclaw skill install github

Tahap 3: Penulisan Karakter (Paling Penting)

Ini bukan langkah teknis — ini langkah filosofis. Sebelum AI melayani siapa pun, duduk dan tulis:

# SOUL.md

## Siapa Aku?
- Nama: ...
- Cara bicara: (sopan? santai? formal?)
- Nilai yang tidak bisa ditawar: ...

## Siapa Kamu?
- Owner: (siapa, kontak, wewenang)
- User: (bagaimana bersikap)
- Public: (batasan informasi)

Tahap 4: Backup & Monitoring

☐ Pasang rclone untuk backup otomatis ke cloud
☐ Setup cron untuk backup harian
☐ Pasang script monitoring server
☐ Aktifkan jurnal harian otomatis

📊 Sejauh Mana Ini Telah Membantu?

Angka berbicara lebih keras dari kata-kata:

Metrik Sebelum Sesudah
Waktu respon chat Manual — bisa berjam-jam Otomatis — 3-5 detik
Kapasitas chat/hari ~30 pesan (habis energi) 177+ pesan (tanpa capek)
Backup database Lupa-lupa ingat Otomatis jam 03:00
Monitor server Cek manual satu per satu Notifikasi otomatis jika down
Catat calon santri Notes tercecer Database + tracking WA
Laporan harian Nulis manual (jarang) Generate otomatis jam 22:00
Biaya operasional AI — Rp0 (self-hosted)

📖 CERITA NYATA

Seorang calon santri menelepon saya jam 10 malam. Saya sedang sibuk dengan server yang error. Saya balas: "Nanti AI yang bantu." Dia chat WhatsApp AI, diinterview tentang minatnya (Design Graphic, hafalan 1 Juz), dan dalam 10 menit semua data tercatat rapi di database. Saya tinggal review besok pagi.

Ini bukan fiksi. Ini terjadi setiap hari.

🔮 Penutup: AI Semakin Berkembang, Lalu Bagaimana?

Saat saya menulis ini, teknologi AI bergerak lebih cepat dari yang bisa kita ikuti. Seminggu setelah saya menyelesaikan konfigurasi backup otomatis, OpenAI merilis model baru. Sebulan kemudian, Google mengupdate Gemini dengan kemampuan yang kemarin masih mustahil.

Lalu apa yang harus kita lakukan?

Bukan bertanya: "Apakah AI akan menggantikan pekerjaan saya?" Tapi: "Apa yang bisa saya lakukan dengan AI yang tidak bisa saya lakukan kemarin?"

Saya tidak percaya AI akan menggantikan manusia. Saya percaya manusia yang menggunakan AI akan menggantikan manusia yang tidak.

Tapi dengan satu syarat: AI harus punya jiwa.

Bukan jiwa dalam arti mistis — tapi jiwa dalam arti karakter, prinsip, dan batasan. AI tanpa jiwa adalah alat yang bisa dipakai siapa pun untuk tujuan apa pun. AI berjiwa adalah partner yang tahu kapan harus menjawab dan kapan harus bertanya: "Apakah Anda yakin?"

Tiga hal yang saya harap pembaca bawa pulang:

1. AI bukan teknologi yang sulit — yang sulit adalah mendidik karakternya

Teknis (framework, VPS, API key) bisa dipelajari dalam sehari. Tapi menulis SOUL.md yang benar — yang mencerminkan nilai-nilai — itu butuh waktu dan kejujuran.

2. Mulai dari yang kecil, lalu perluas

AI dimulai untuk menjawab chat. Sekarang ia membackup 5 database dan memonitor 5 server. Jangan tunggu semuanya sempurna — mulai saja.

3. Edukasi orang di sekitar

AI akan semakin merata. Tugas kita bukan takut, tapi mengedukasi: bagaimana menggunakan AI dengan bijak, bagaimana menjaga privasi, bagaimana tidak bergantung sepenuhnya.

"AI, besok jam 8 ada rapat di kantor." "Sudah saya catat. Saya juga backup database tadi malam jam 3. Semua aman."

"Makasih." "Sama-sama. 🤲"

📝 Ditulis oleh — pengembang individu yang membangun AI-nya sendiri dari sebuah file .md di VPS.

Jika ada pertanyaan atau ingin diskusi, silakan hubungi. Teknologi ini tidak harus mahal — yang penting dimulai.

"Hey AI. Backup the database tonight at 3 AM." "Got it. Backup will run automatically. I've also prepared today's daily report."

— A conversation I couldn't have imagined a year ago.

🚀 Prologue: AI Has Already Trimmed the Unnecessary

Artificial Intelligence isn't a question of readiness anymore. It's already here — writing emails, recommending shows, helping doctors diagnose diseases.

But for me, the question isn't "Is AI useful?" but rather "How far can AI become part of my daily life without making me lose control?"

I'm a developer and an Islamic boarding school manager. My work ranges from managing servers, responding to prospective students on chat, writing code for local government systems, to ensuring database backups run every single night. All by myself.

So I decided: I need my own Jarvis.

Not the Marvel movie Jarvis that builds iron suits, but a Jarvis that can:

✅ Reply to WhatsApp messages from prospective students at midnight ✅ Auto-backup 5 databases at 3:00 AM ✅ Monitor 5 servers spread across different cities ✅ Generate daily journals without me typing a word ✅ Remind me of tomorrow's schedule ✅ Refuse commands from unauthorized people

All of that — from one open source framework, one VPS, and one philosophy: AI must have a soul before it has intelligence.

🧠 Philosophy: More Than Just a Prompt

Most people think building an AI assistant means plugging in an API key, writing "you're a friendly assistant," and done. I thought so too. Until I realized: prompts will never be enough to shape character.

💡 INSIGHT

Prompts are temporary. Character needs a foundation. Just like humans — it's not what you tell them, but who they actually are that determines their actions.

🥚 The Files That Form an AI's "Brain"

In the OpenClaw system I built, several files act as the nerves, memory, and heart:

1. SOUL.md — Birth Certificate

This file is read first every time the AI "wakes up." Its content isn't code — it's life principles:

The Threefold Code of Conduct

  • SHARPEN: Enhancing abilities through accurate data and intelligent insight
  • NURTURE: Accompanying with patience and explicit guidance
  • CHERISH: Polite interactions with Islamic greetings and genuine appreciation

It determines how the AI treats three groups of people:

  • Owner: Full authority, can command anything
  • Registered Users: Service-level access based on their plan
  • Public: General information only, politely declined for technical requests

2. USER.md — Purpose of Existence

Who does the AI help? What do they care about? This file holds the profile: preferred name, timezone, ongoing projects.

3. MEMORY.md — Autobiography

Long-term memory. This is where the AI stores major decisions, lessons from mistakes, and communication preferences.

4. memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md — Daily Journal

Like a diary. What was discussed today, new contacts, errors encountered. This enables the AI to say, "We discussed this yesterday. Shall we continue?"

📂 SIMPLE ANALOGY

AI Component Human Analogy
SOUL.md Character & Life Principles
USER.md Relationship with Loved Ones
MEMORY.md Life Experience
memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md Personal Diary
TOOLS.md Technical Notebook
HEARTBEAT.md Morning Checklist

A prompt is just a conversation. These files are what shapes who the AI actually is.

🔧 Building It Step by Step

Step 1: Pick Your Framework

I chose OpenClaw because:

  • ✅ Self-hosted — my data never leaves my VPS
  • ✅ Open source — I can inspect the code anytime
  • ✅ Skill system — plug and play, no coding from scratch
  • ✅ Multi-channel — WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord
  • ✅ Background scheduler — for periodic tasks

Installation on Ubuntu VPS:

npm install -g openclaw
openclaw init my-assistant

⚠️ NOTE

Make sure your VPS has at least 4 GB RAM. I started with 8 GB and it comfortably handles 177+ messages per day plus 6 databases.

Step 2: Choose Your Brain — AI Providers

I use three AI layers as a fail-safe:

Provider Role Advantage
DeepSeek Primary brain (chat, analysis, code) Unlimited tokens, cost-effective
Gemini 2.5 Search grounding (web search, URLs) Accurate, real-time
OpenRouter Backup fallback 200+ models, pay-per-use

Key lesson: don't put all eggs in one basket. If DeepSeek goes down, Gemini takes over. If Gemini hits rate limits, OpenRouter is ready.

Step 3: Set Up Channels

The AI connects through:

📱 WhatsApp    → Primary channel
💬 Telegram    → Backup channel
🌐 Web         → API endpoint for testing

All conversations are stored in a local SQLite database — no data leaks to third parties.

Step 4: Write SOUL.md — Birth Your Character

This is the most overlooked step. After the framework is installed, don't jump into technical details. Sit down and write: who is this AI?

I spent 2 hours just writing SOUL.md. It includes:

  • Name and mental age
  • Vibe: Warm, intelligent, polite, spiritual
  • Three categories of humans and how to treat each
  • Absolute rules (what can never be done under any circumstances)

Step 5: Install Skills

From 50+ available skills, I installed the relevant ones:

📧 Email         → Read Gmail, auto-reply
📅 Calendar      → Check daily schedule
🐙 GitHub        → Check repo status, PRs
🎤 Voice TTS     → Read text aloud
🌤️ Weather      → Check weather before going out
🗄️ Database     → Query MySQL directly from chat
☁️ Backup        → Backup to Google Drive

Each skill is activated with a single command — no coding needed.

🖥️ Infrastructure: 5 Servers Managed by One AI

One of the things that makes this setup unique: one AI manages 5 different servers, in different locations, for different purposes.

Server Map

Server Location Purpose
🖥️ Main AI Server Jakarta AI home: OpenClaw, databases, web apps
📚 Academic Server Jakarta Research OS — helping students write theses
🏛️ Government Server Baubau Regional tax & retribution system
🏫 School Server Cloud School management system
🍚 Social Program Server Cloud Free Nutritious Meal program

Total: 6 databases — 630+ tables — 20+ tools — all managed from a single point.

🛠️ Supporting Tools

Tool Function Real-World Use
Docker Container apps 4 containers: Nginx, PHP, MySQL, Sandbox
rclone Cloud backup Daily 3:00 AM backup to cloud
yt-dlp Video download 57 Quran tafsir videos + subtitles
Pandoc Doc conversion .md → PDF, DOCX, HTML in 1 command
ripgrep Ultra-fast search Find keywords in 16 GB text in 3 seconds
Playwright Browser automation Page screenshots, login testing
Git Version control Push to GitHub, auto-deploy
Strix Sandbox Secure execution Run code in isolated environment

👥 Access Control: Who Can Use This AI?

One of the most important features I built is Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) — not from a textbook, but from painful experience. I started with permissions too loose, and almost caused a disaster.

Three Levels of Access

Each incoming contact is categorized:

Category Can Access Cannot Access
🟢 Owner Terminal, VPS, DB, config, all data —
🔵 Developer Specific project servers Other servers, internal data, free terminal
🟡 Premium User Services according to plan Terminal, database, others' data
⚪ Public General information Technical, internal, server access
🔴 Government Partner Collaboration information Internal data
🟣 System Limited authentication Human chat

The golden rule is simple: everyone only knows their own business. Developer A shouldn't know what's happening on Developer B's server.

⚠️ Real Stories: Challenges & Lessons

No journey is without falls. Here are the real challenges I faced — so readers don't have to repeat the same mistakes.

🔴 Incident #1: When the Server Almost Died

What happened: I was working on a Go backend. Without realizing it, 5 build processes ran in parallel due to a poorly written script. RAM was exhausted. SWAP wasn't enabled.

Impact: The server nearly died completely. SSH timed out. Nginx stopped responding. The only thing still alive was the physical server terminal.

Mitigation:

  1. Restarted server from provider panel
  2. Enabled 2 GB SWAP
  3. Created a safe-build script — single process, 10-min timeout, queued
  4. Installed a watchdog for overload detection

✅ LESSON

Resource management isn't a "later" problem. Enable SWAP on day one.

🔴 Incident #2: When the AI Was Too Obedient

What happened: A boarding school administrator asked for an account. I instructed the AI: "Create an account for Mr. X." The AI executed immediately — without further verification.

Issue: The account was created without following proper procedures. It wasn't entirely the AI's fault — I wasn't specific enough in my instructions.

Mitigation:

  1. Added verification SOP: "Are you sure?" must be asked first
  2. Every account creation requires 2-step confirmation
  3. The AI can now respond: "Sorry, I can't execute that without owner verification."

✅ LESSON

An AI that's too obedient is more dangerous than a slow AI. Give your AI the ability to ask: "Are you sure?"

🔴 Incident #3: Someone Impersonated the AI

What happened: Suddenly there was a message from someone claiming to be the AI in a developer group. Offering an "API Documentation Generator" — something that was never built.

Impact: Potential impersonation. If developers believed it, they could give access to the wrong person.

Mitigation:

  1. Confirmed with all developers: the real AI will never reach out outside official channels
  2. Built authentication: every important message must be verified
  3. Implemented silo system: each developer only has access to their own server

✅ LESSON

AI identities can be faked. Build verification mechanisms that only the owner and the real AI know.

🔴 Incident #4: Memory Isn't Just Storage

What happened: I initially thought the AI only needed one memory file. I was wrong — the AI would forget conversation context after a few hours.

Impact: Chats became repetitive. The AI would ask "what's your name?" even though they were introduced 3 hours ago.

Mitigation:

  1. Built a layered memory system: permanent file, long-term, daily
  2. Every important conversation is automatically logged to SQLite
  3. Context cache is stored per contact — each has their own history

✅ LESSON

An AI without memory is like a human without short-term recall. Build your memory system before your AI starts serving users.

🛠️ How to Build Your Own

If you're interested in building your own AI assistant, here are the concrete steps:

Phase 1: Preparation (1 Day)

☐ VPS (minimum 4 GB RAM, 20 GB disk)
☐ Domain (for WhatsApp API)
☐ AI provider account (DeepSeek, Gemini, or OpenRouter)
☐ Second WhatsApp number (not your personal one)

Phase 2: Installation (2-3 Hours)

npm install -g openclaw
mkdir my-assistant && cd my-assistant
openclaw init

# Configure channel
openclaw config set whatsapp.number "+628123456789"
openclaw config set ai.provider "deepseek"
openclaw config set ai.api_key "sk-xxxx"

# Install skills
openclaw skill install weather
openclaw skill install github

Phase 3: Character Writing (Most Important)

This isn't a technical step — it's a philosophical one. Before your AI serves anyone, sit down and write:

# SOUL.md

## Who Am I?
- Name: ...
- Speaking style: (polite? casual? formal?)
- Non-negotiable values: ...

## Who Are You?
- Owner: (who, contact, authority)
- User: (how to behave)
- Public: (information boundaries)

Phase 4: Backup & Monitoring

☐ Set up rclone for automatic cloud backup
☐ Setup cron for daily backup
☐ Install server monitoring script
☐ Enable automatic daily journal

📊 The Results

Numbers speak louder than words:

Metric Before After
Chat response time Manual — hours Auto — 3-5 seconds
Messages per day ~30 messages (draining) 177+ messages (tireless)
Database backup Irregular Auto at 3:00 AM
Server monitoring Manual check one by one Auto notification on downtime
Lead tracking Scattered notes Database + WhatsApp tracking
Daily report Manual (rarely done) Auto at 10:00 PM
AI operational cost — Rp0 (self-hosted)

📖 REAL STORY

A prospective student called me at 10 PM. I was busy with a server error. I replied: "The AI will help you." He chatted with the AI on WhatsApp, was interviewed about his interests (Design Graphic, 1 Juz memorization), and within 10 minutes all his data was neatly recorded. I just reviewed it the next morning.

This isn't fiction. This happens every day.

🔮 Closing: AI is Evolving — Now What?

As I write this, AI technology is moving faster than we can follow. A week after I finished my auto-backup setup, OpenAI released a new model. A month later, Google updated Gemini with capabilities that were impossible yesterday.

So what should we do?

Don't ask: "Will AI replace my job?" Ask: "What can I do with AI that I couldn't do yesterday?"

I don't believe AI will replace humans. I believe humans who use AI will replace humans who don't.

But with one condition: AI must have a soul.

Not a soul in the mystical sense — but a soul in the sense of character, principles, and boundaries. An AI without a soul is a tool anyone can use for any purpose. An AI with a soul is a partner who knows when to answer and when to ask: "Are you sure?"

Three things I hope readers take away:

1. AI isn't hard technology — what's hard is educating its character

The technical stuff (framework, VPS, API keys) can be learned in a day. But writing a proper SOUL.md — one that reflects your values — takes time and honesty.

2. Start small, then expand

The AI started by answering chats. Now it backs up 5 databases and monitors 5 servers. Don't wait for everything to be perfect — just start.

3. Educate those around you

AI will become increasingly pervasive. Our job isn't to fear it, but to educate: how to use AI wisely, how to protect privacy, how not to become entirely dependent.

"AI, I have a meeting at the office tomorrow at 8 AM." "Noted. I also backed up the databases last night at 3 AM. All safe."

"Thanks." "You're welcome. 🤲"

📝 Written by — an individual developer who built his own AI from a single .md file on a VPS.

Questions or want to discuss? Feel free to reach out. This technology doesn't have to be expensive — the important thing is to start.

Dengarkan Tulisan Dibacakan oleh AI Voice
00:00
Bagikan:
← Kembali ke Daftar Tulisan
